_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_PLAYER: 1. Open Gemini (gemini.google.com) or any AI chat
2. Upload or paste this entire file
3. Tell the AI what characters you want, for example:
'Fill in these 3 characters for Hoard of the Dragon Queen:
PC1: A human fighter soldier who uses a greatsword
PC2: A half-elf bard entertainer who talks her way out of everything
PC3: A high elf wizard sage obsessed with ancient dragon lore'
4. The AI will fill in all the stats, spells, equipment, and features
5. Copy the JSON output the AI gives you (everything between the curly braces)
6. In the app: go to Systems tab > Dev > Paste JSON area
7. Paste it in and tap Import — your characters will load into the game
- You can ask for ANY race/class combo from D&D 5e
- Ask the AI to roll stats or use point buy — your call
- If something looks wrong, ask the AI to fix it before importing
- The AI should NOT include the _INSTRUCTIONS fields in its output
- If you only want to update 1 or 2 characters, that's fine — the app matches by id
_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_AI: INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE AI FILLING THIS OUT:
Fill in 3 Level 1 D&D 5e characters for Hoard of the Dragon Queen.
Use the EXACT JSON structure below — do not add or remove any fields.
Replace all placeholder values with real D&D 5e data.
RULES:
- Keep id fields exactly as pc1, pc2, pc3
- Keep color fields as-is (#c04a3a, #4a7090, #7060a0)
- Stats format: '16 (+3)' — always include the modifier in parens
- HP must match class hit die + CON modifier (Fighter d10, Wizard d6, Bard d8, etc.)
- AC must reflect actual armor worn (leather=11+DEX, chain mail=16, etc.)
- initiative = DEX modifier number only (e.g. 2 for DEX 14)
- passive_perception = 10 + WIS modifier + proficiency if proficient in Perception
- passive_insight = 10 + WIS modifier + proficiency if proficient in Insight
- skills: list ALL proficient skills with correct modifiers, e.g. 'Athletics +5, Perception +3'
- features: ALL class + racial features in CAPS with full descriptions, separated by newlines
- magic: empty string for non-casters. For casters use: 'Spellcasting: [Ability] | Save DC: [N] | Attack Bonus: +[N]\nCantrips: ...\nLevel 1 Spells: ...'
- slots: [] for non-casters. For casters: [{'max': 2, 'used': 0}] for each spell level
- resources: trackable per-rest abilities like Second Wind, Bardic Inspiration, etc. Format: {'name':'...','max':N,'used':0,'restore':'short' or 'long','desc':'...'}
- inventory: starting equipment with weights. type = weapon/armor/supply/tool/etc. Include notes with damage dice, properties
- skillProfs: array of proficient saving throws, e.g. ['STR Save', 'CON Save']
- languages: array of known languages, e.g. ['Common', 'Dwarvish']
OUTPUT: Return ONLY the JSON with the pcs array (no _INSTRUCTIONS fields). No markdown code fences, no commentary — just raw JSON.
The player will paste your output into their D&D campaign app at Systems > Dev > Paste JSON

pcs.name: CHARACTER NAME
pcs.race: Race (e.g. Human, Dwarf, Elf, Tiefling, Dragonborn)
pcs.skills: List all proficient skills with modifiers, e.g. Athletics +5, Perception +3, Stealth +4
pcs.features:
ALL CLASS AND RACIAL FEATURES in caps with descriptions, separated by \n. Example:
FIGHTING STYLE (Defense): +1 AC while wearing armor.
SECOND WIND (Bonus Action, 1/Short Rest): Regain 1d10+1 HP.
pcs.magic:
Leave empty for non-casters. For casters use format:
Spellcasting: [Ability] | Save DC: [N] | Attack Bonus: +[N]
Cantrips: Spell1, Spell2
Level 1 Spells: Spell1, Spell2, Spell3
